title: Configure payment webhook calls to Zapier endpoint

requirements.summary: Display the configured Zapier webhook URL in the admin payments panel so admins can verify the active payment webhook endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: Admin payment panel shows the active Zapier webhook URL: https://hooks.zapier.com/hooks/catch/26632326/u0i6cx6/
The field is read-only and clearly labeled as 'Payment Webhook Endpoint'
The display is visible within the Payments tab of the Admin Center

file_operations.description: Add a read-only info section at the top of PaymentsPanel displaying the Zapier webhook URL (https://hooks.zapier.com/hooks/catch/26632326/u0i6cx6/) as 'Payment Webhook Endpoint'. Use a Card or Alert component with an info icon, clear label, and the full URL rendered in a monospace font. Position it above the existing payout form so admins can verify the configuration before making payments.

requirements.summary: Fix Admin Center access control so the authenticated owner/admin user is recognized and granted admin access, resolving the 'Admin Access Required' lock screen
acceptanceCriteria: The authenticated admin user can access the Admin Center without seeing the lock screen
Non-admin users still see the 'Admin Access Required' lock screen
Access control state is correctly initialized when AdminCenterPage mounts

file_operations.description: Review the access control initialization logic in AdminCenterPage. Currently the page calls initializeAccessControl on mount and checks isCallerAdmin. Ensure that: (1) the initializeAccessControl mutation is awaited or its success is monitored before rendering the lock screen, (2) the isCallerAdmin query is enabled only after the actor is available and the user is authenticated, (3) loading states for both the initialization and the admin check are properly handled so the lock screen does not flash prematurely, and (4) the condition rendering the lock screen waits for isFetched from the isCallerAdmin query. Add error handling for initialization failures. Verify the component's usage instructions for the authorization component before implementing.

Review and ensure the useIsCallerAdmin query hook is correctly configured with enabled: !!actor && !actorFetching, and that it exposes both isLoading and isFetched states. If the query is incorrectly enabled or missing dependency checks, update it to mirror the pattern used in useGetCallerUserProfile (check actor availability and actorFetching before enabling). This ensures AdminCenterPage can reliably determine when the admin check has completed.
